name: Siauliai County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
silver field of the county coat of arms

description: A blue field with a silver inner panel bearing the coat of arms of Siauliai County in the centre.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Siauliai County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue outer border surrounding the county's heraldic field, here silver, charged with the county arms.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, with a silver inner field bearing the county arms

features.role: coat of arms of Siauliai County
features.symbolism: a black bear and other heraldic emblems on a silver field, referencing the regional arms of Siauliai
